holidays.name: National Peanut Butter Lovers Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to celebrating the creamy or crunchy spread that has been a pantry staple for generations.
holidays.funFact: Americans consume about 700 million pounds of peanut butter every year.
holidays.shareText: Spreading the love on National Peanut Butter Lovers Day! 🥜 #PeanutButterLoversDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Compliment Day
holidays.description: A global day encouraging people to give sincere and meaningful compliments to those around them.
holidays.funFact: Research shows that receiving a genuine compliment activates the same reward region of the brain as receiving cash.
holidays.shareText: Spreading kind words today for World Compliment Day! 😊 #WorldComplimentDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Dr. Seuss Day
holidays.description: Celebrated on the birthday of Theodor Seuss Geisel, this day honors the beloved author who inspired generations of young readers.
holidays.funFact: Dr. Seuss wrote The Cat in the Hat using only 236 different words to help children learn to read.
holidays.shareText: Today I am reading and I think it is great! Happy Dr. Seuss Day! 📚 #DrSeussDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Banana Cream Pie Day
holidays.description: A sweet celebration of the classic American dessert featuring creamy custard, fresh bananas, and whipped topping.
holidays.funFact: Banana cream pie became popular in the United States in the early 1900s as bananas became more widely available.
holidays.shareText: Digging into a slice of heaven for National Banana Cream Pie Day! 🥧 #BananaCreamPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Wildlife Day
holidays.description: A United Nations observance day to celebrate and raise awareness of the world's wild animals and plants.
holidays.funFact: Over 8.7 million species are estimated to exist on Earth, but scientists have only formally identified about 1.2 million of them.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the wild wonders of our planet on World Wildlife Day! 🐾 #WorldWildlifeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Anthem Day
holidays.description: A day commemorating the moment the Star Spangled Banner was officially adopted as the national anthem of the United States in 1931.
holidays.funFact: The Star Spangled Banner has four verses, but most Americans only know the first one.
holidays.shareText: Standing proud and singing loud on National Anthem Day! 🎶 #NationalAnthemDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Grammar Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate and promote the proper use of the English language in writing and speech.
holidays.funFact: The word grammar comes from the Greek word grammatike, meaning the art of letters.
holidays.shareText: Using all the right words on National Grammar Day! ✏️ #NationalGrammarDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Hug a GI Day
holidays.description: A day to show appreciation and gratitude for the men and women who serve or have served in the military.
holidays.funFact: The term GI originally stood for galvanized iron, referring to military equipment, before becoming slang for soldiers.
holidays.shareText: Showing love and gratitude for our military heroes on Hug a GI Day! 🎖️ #HugAGIDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cheese Doodle Day
holidays.description: A fun food holiday honoring the irresistibly crunchy and cheesy puffed snack loved by kids and adults alike.
holidays.funFact: Cheese puffs were accidentally invented in the 1930s when a machine used to process animal feed created puffed corn pieces.
holidays.shareText: My fingers are orange and I have zero regrets! Happy National Cheese Doodle Day! 🧀 #CheeseDoodleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Multiple Personality Day
holidays.description: A lighthearted day that celebrates the many different sides of a person's personality and the complexity of human character.
holidays.funFact: The term multiple personality disorder was officially renamed dissociative identity disorder by the American Psychiatric Association in 1994.
holidays.shareText: Embracing all my many sides today on Multiple Personality Day! 🎭 #MultiplePersonalityDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Oreo Cookie Day
holidays.description: A sweet tribute to one of the best selling cookies in the world, first introduced by Nabisco in 1912.
holidays.funFact: More than 450 billion Oreo cookies have been sold since they were first introduced, making them the best selling cookie of the 20th century.
holidays.shareText: Twist, lick, dunk! Celebrating National Oreo Cookie Day! 🍪 #NationalOreoCookieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Dentists Day
holidays.description: A day to appreciate the dental professionals who keep our smiles healthy and bright all year long.
holidays.funFact: Ancient Egyptians practiced a form of dentistry as far back as 2600 BCE, with evidence of tooth fillings made from a paste of minerals.
holidays.shareText: Smiling big and thanking my dentist on National Dentists Day! 🦷 #NationalDentistsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Be Heard Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging individuals and small businesses to speak up, promote themselves, and make their voices count.
holidays.funFact: The average person speaks approximately 16,000 words per day, but only a fraction of those words are used to advocate for themselves.
holidays.shareText: Using my voice and making it count on National Be Heard Day! 📣 #NationalBeHeardDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Crown Roast of Pork Day
holidays.description: A celebration of one of the most impressive and elegant presentations in home cooking, the magnificent crown roast.
holidays.funFact: A crown roast is formed by tying two rib racks together in a circle, and can contain anywhere from 16 to 20 ribs.
holidays.shareText: Feeling fancy with a crown roast on National Crown Roast of Pork Day! 🍖 #CrownRoastOfPorkDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Womens Day
holidays.description: A global day celebrating the social, economic, cultural, and political achievements of women around the world.
holidays.funFact: International Womens Day has been observed since the early 1900s, with the first official celebration held on March 19, 1911.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the incredible women who make our world better every day! ♀️ #InternationalWomensDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Peanut Cluster Day
holidays.description: A delicious day honoring the satisfying combination of crunchy peanuts and rich chocolate molded into irresistible clusters.
holidays.funFact: Peanuts are actually legumes, not true nuts, and are more closely related to beans and lentils than to almonds or cashews.
holidays.shareText: Treating myself to some chocolatey goodness on National Peanut Cluster Day! 🍫 #PeanutClusterDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Meatball Day
holidays.description: A savory celebration of the beloved spherical meat dish that has been a comfort food staple across many cultures.
holidays.funFact: Meatballs appear in cuisines all around the world, from Italian polpette to Swedish kottbullar to Middle Eastern kofta.
holidays.shareText: Rolling into the day with pure joy on National Meatball Day! 🍝 #NationalMeatballDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Barbies Birthday
holidays.description: Celebrating the birthday of the iconic Barbie doll, which debuted at the American International Toy Fair on March 9, 1959.
holidays.funFact: Barbies full name is Barbara Millicent Roberts, and she has held over 200 careers since her debut in 1959.
holidays.shareText: Wishing a happy birthday to a true icon on Barbies Birthday! 👗 #BarbiesBirthday #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pack Your Lunch Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging people to skip the takeout and bring a homemade lunch to work or school for health and savings.
holidays.funFact: The average American who buys lunch spends about 2,500 dollars per year eating out, compared to roughly 500 dollars for those who pack their own.
holidays.shareText: Packing my lunch and saving money on National Pack Your Lunch Day! 🥪 #PackYourLunchDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Mario Day
holidays.description: Celebrated because MAR10 looks like Mario, this day honors Nintendo's iconic mustachioed plumber and gaming legend.
holidays.funFact: Mario has appeared in over 200 video games since his debut in Donkey Kong in 1981, making him one of the most recognizable characters in entertainment history.
holidays.shareText: Its a me, celebrating Mario Day! 🎮 #MarioDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Oatmeal Nut Waffles Day
holidays.description: A breakfast celebration honoring the hearty and delicious combination of oats and nuts baked into a perfectly crispy waffle.
holidays.funFact: Waffles have been made since the Middle Ages, with the distinctive grid pattern originating from wafer irons used in the 13th century.
holidays.shareText: Starting the morning right with oatmeal nut waffles! 🧇 #OatmealNutWafflesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Johnny Appleseed Day
holidays.description: A tribute to John Chapman, the American folk hero who planted apple trees across the Midwest in the early 1800s.
holidays.funFact: Johnny Appleseed planted apple orchards across more than 100,000 square miles of land throughout Ohio, Indiana, and Illinois.
holidays.shareText: Planting seeds of kindness on Johnny Appleseed Day! 🍎 #JohnnyAppleseedDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Plant a Flower Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging people to get their hands in the soil and plant flowers to welcome the coming spring season.
holidays.funFact: Flowers have been cultivated by humans for at least 5,000 years, with ancient Egyptians being among the first to use them decoratively.
holidays.shareText: Getting my hands dirty and making the world prettier on National Plant a Flower Day! 🌸 #PlantAFlowerDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Girl Scout Day
holidays.description: Commemorating the founding of the Girl Scouts of the USA by Juliette Gordon Low on March 12, 1912.
holidays.funFact: The Girl Scouts of the USA was founded with just 18 girls in Savannah, Georgia, and has grown to more than 2.5 million members today.
holidays.shareText: Honoring the amazing Girl Scouts and their legacy on Girl Scout Day! 🍪 #GirlScoutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Coconut Torte Day
holidays.description: A celebration of the rich and elegant coconut torte, a layered dessert featuring the tropical flavor of sweet coconut.
holidays.funFact: Coconuts are technically a fruit, a nut, and a seed all at the same time, making them one of nature's most versatile foods.
holidays.shareText: Indulging in a tropical treat on National Coconut Torte Day! 🥥 #CoconutTorteDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Good Samaritan Day
holidays.description: A day inspired by the biblical parable, encouraging acts of kindness and helping strangers in need.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that people who regularly perform acts of kindness report higher levels of happiness and life satisfaction than those who do not.
holidays.shareText: Paying it forward and being a Good Samaritan today! 🤝 #GoodSamaritanDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Pi Day
holidays.description: A celebration of the mathematical constant pi (3.14...), observed annually on 3/14 by math enthusiasts around the world.
holidays.funFact: Pi has been calculated to over 100 trillion digits beyond the decimal point, yet only 39 digits are needed to calculate the circumference of the observable universe.
holidays.shareText: Going around in circles celebrating Pi Day! 🥧 #PiDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Potato Chip Day
holidays.description: A crunchy celebration of Americas favorite snack, which was accidentally invented in 1853 by chef George Crum.
holidays.funFact: Americans consume about 1.85 billion pounds of potato chips per year, making it the most popular snack food in the United States.
holidays.shareText: Crunching away happily on National Potato Chip Day! 🥔 #NationalPotatoChipDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: The Ides of March
holidays.description: A date made famous by the assassination of Julius Caesar in 44 BCE, now a cultural touchstone in history and literature.
holidays.funFact: In the Roman calendar, the Ides simply referred to the middle of the month, and was a completely ordinary day before Julius Caesar's assassination.
holidays.shareText: Beware the Ides of March! Embracing a legendary day in history! 🏛️ #IdesOfMarch #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Consumer Rights Day
holidays.description: A global day to promote consumer rights and raise awareness of the need to protect consumers from marketplace abuses.
holidays.funFact: World Consumer Rights Day has been observed since 1983, inspired by US President John F. Kennedy's 1962 speech to Congress on consumer rights.
holidays.shareText: Standing up for fair treatment and consumer rights today! 🛡️ #WorldConsumerRightsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Artichoke Hearts Day
holidays.description: A culinary celebration of the tender and flavorful heart of the artichoke, a prized ingredient in kitchens around the world.
holidays.funFact: Artichokes are actually the flower bud of a thistle plant, and if left unpicked they bloom into a stunning large purple flower.
holidays.shareText: Getting to the heart of the matter on National Artichoke Hearts Day! 🌿 #ArtichokeHeartsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Freedom of Information Day
holidays.description: A day marking the birthday of James Madison, celebrating open government and the public's right to access information.
holidays.funFact: The US Freedom of Information Act, signed in 1966, allows any person to request access to federal agency records or information.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating transparency and the right to know on Freedom of Information Day! 📰 #FreedomOfInformationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: St. Patricks Day
holidays.description: A cultural and religious celebration honoring the patron saint of Ireland, filled with parades, green attire, and Irish pride.
holidays.funFact: The first St. Patricks Day parade in the United States was held in Boston in 1737, predating the American Revolution.
holidays.shareText: Wearing green and celebrating the luck of the Irish on St. Patricks Day! ☘️ #StPatricksDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Corned Beef and Cabbage Day
holidays.description: A traditional St. Patricks Day feast featuring salt cured beef brisket slow cooked with cabbage and hearty vegetables.
holidays.funFact: Corned beef and cabbage as a St. Patricks Day dish is largely an Irish American tradition; in Ireland, the traditional dish is more commonly bacon and cabbage.
holidays.shareText: Digging into a classic feast on National Corned Beef and Cabbage Day! 🥩 #CornedBeefAndCabbageDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Awkward Moments Day
holidays.description: A lighthearted day to laugh at and embrace the cringeworthy, embarrassing moments that make life hilariously relatable.
holidays.funFact: Research shows that people who can laugh at themselves and their awkward moments tend to have stronger social connections and greater resilience.
holidays.shareText: Laughing at myself and all life's awkward moments today! 😬 #NationalAwkwardMomentsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Lacy Oatmeal Cookie Day
holidays.description: A sweet celebration of the delicate, thin, and crispy lacy oatmeal cookie with its caramelized, lacework appearance.
holidays.funFact: Lacy oatmeal cookies get their distinctive see through appearance from the high sugar and butter content, which spreads and caramelizes during baking.
holidays.shareText: Baking up something beautiful on Lacy Oatmeal Cookie Day! 🍪 #LacyOatmealCookieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Poultry Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating all types of domesticated birds raised for food, from chicken and turkey to duck and quail.
holidays.funFact: Chickens are the most numerous bird on the planet, with a global population of over 33 billion, outnumbering humans nearly four to one.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating our feathered food friends on National Poultry Day! 🐔 #NationalPoultryDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lets Laugh Day
holidays.description: A joyful day dedicated to sharing laughter, telling jokes, and finding the humor in everyday life.
holidays.funFact: Laughter has genuine health benefits, including reducing stress hormones, boosting the immune system, and even burning calories.
holidays.shareText: Ha ha ha! Today is all about the laughs on National Lets Laugh Day! 😂 #NationalLetsLaughDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Frog Day
holidays.description: A global day raising awareness about the conservation of frogs and the threats they face in their natural habitats.
holidays.funFact: There are over 7,000 known species of frogs on Earth, and scientists continue to discover new species each year in remote rainforests.
holidays.shareText: Jumping for joy and protecting our amphibian friends on World Frog Day! 🐸 #WorldFrogDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: First Day of Spring
holidays.description: The vernal equinox marks the astronomical beginning of spring, when day and night are nearly equal in length across the globe.
holidays.funFact: On the spring equinox, the sun rises due east and sets due west everywhere on Earth, regardless of your location.
holidays.shareText: Hello sunshine and blooming flowers! Happy First Day of Spring! 🌱 #FirstDayOfSpring #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Down Syndrome Day
holidays.description: A global awareness day for Down syndrome, chosen for the uniqueness of the triplication of the 21st chromosome.
holidays.funFact: World Down Syndrome Day is observed on 3/21 to represent the unique triplication of the 21st chromosome that causes Down syndrome.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating abilities and spreading awareness on World Down Syndrome Day! 💛 #WorldDownSyndromeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National French Bread Day
holidays.description: A crusty and delicious celebration of the iconic French baguette and the rich tradition of French bread baking.
holidays.funFact: French law actually regulates what can be called a traditional baguette, requiring it to be made with only four ingredients: flour, water, salt, and yeast.
holidays.shareText: Oui oui, celebrating the perfect loaf on National French Bread Day! 🥖 #FrenchBreadDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Water Day
holidays.description: A United Nations observance day focusing on the importance of freshwater and advocating for sustainable management of water resources.
holidays.funFact: Only 3 percent of Earths water is fresh water, and of that, about 2.5 percent is locked in ice caps and glaciers.
holidays.shareText: Every drop counts! Celebrating and conserving water on World Water Day! 💧 #WorldWaterDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Goof Off Day
holidays.description: A playful day giving everyone official permission to take a break, have fun, and not take life too seriously.
holidays.funFact: Taking regular breaks and engaging in playful activities has been shown to boost creativity, productivity, and overall mental well being.
holidays.shareText: Officially goofing off today and loving every minute on National Goof Off Day! 🤪 #NationalGoofOffDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Puppy Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the pure joy that puppies bring to our lives and to raise awareness about puppy adoption.
holidays.funFact: Puppies are born blind, deaf, and toothless, and they spend about 90 percent of their first weeks of life sleeping.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the fluffiest, most adorable beings on the planet on National Puppy Day! 🐶 #NationalPuppyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Meteorological Day
holidays.description: A day marking the establishment of the World Meteorological Organization in 1950 and celebrating weather science around the globe.
holidays.funFact: The WMO coordinates a global network of more than 10,000 weather stations, 1,000 upper air stations, and 7,000 ship and buoy observation systems.
holidays.shareText: Looking up at the sky and appreciating the science of weather on World Meteorological Day! 🌤️ #WorldMeteorologicalDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Tuberculosis Day
holidays.description: A day to raise public awareness about the devastating health, social, and economic consequences of tuberculosis.
holidays.funFact: TB is one of the oldest known diseases in human history, with evidence of the infection found in Egyptian mummies dating back 3,000 years.
holidays.shareText: Raising awareness and supporting the fight to end tuberculosis on World TB Day! 🎗️ #WorldTuberculosisDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cocktail Day
holidays.description: A spirited celebration of the art of mixology and the creative cocktail culture that has flourished around the world.
holidays.funFact: The word cocktail first appeared in print in a New York newspaper in 1806, defined as a stimulating liquor composed of spirits, sugar, water, and bitters.
holidays.shareText: Mixing up something amazing on National Cocktail Day! 🍹 #NationalCocktailDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Tolkien Reading Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the works of J.R.R. Tolkien by encouraging fans to read and share passages from his beloved books.
holidays.funFact: Tolkien created entire languages for his fictional worlds, including Quenya and Sindarin for the Elves, each with its own grammar and vocabulary.
holidays.shareText: Journeying to Middle Earth today on Tolkien Reading Day! 📖 #TolkienReadingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Waffle Day
holidays.description: A beloved Scandinavian tradition celebrating waffles, originating in Sweden where waffle day coincides with the Feast of the Annunciation.
holidays.funFact: The waffle iron was invented in the 14th century, and Thomas Jefferson is credited with bringing a waffle iron back to the United States from France in the 1790s.
holidays.shareText: Stacking up the joy on International Waffle Day! 🧇 #InternationalWaffleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Make Up Your Own Holiday Day
holidays.description: The most creative holiday of the year, giving everyone the freedom to invent and celebrate whatever kind of holiday they want.
holidays.funFact: There are thousands of unofficial holidays celebrated throughout the year, many of which were created by individuals, businesses, or food companies to promote awareness or products.
holidays.shareText: Today I declare it National Awesome Day because I said so! Happy Make Up Your Own Holiday Day! 🎉 #MakeUpYourOwnHolidayDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Spinach Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the leafy green superfood packed with vitamins, minerals, and nutritional goodness.
holidays.funFact: Popeye the Sailor Man was so influential in promoting spinach that spinach consumption in the United States increased by 33 percent during the 1930s.
holidays.shareText: Eating my spinach and feeling strong on National Spinach Day! 🥬 #NationalSpinachDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Theatre Day
holidays.description: An international celebration of the art of theatre in all its forms, founded by the International Theatre Institute in 1961.
holidays.funFact: Theatre is one of the oldest art forms in the world, with the ancient Greeks performing plays in stone amphitheatres as early as the 5th century BCE.
holidays.shareText: The stage is set for a fantastic day on World Theatre Day! 🎭 #WorldTheatreDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Spanish Paella Day
holidays.description: A flavorful celebration of Spain's most iconic dish, a saffron infused rice dish traditionally cooked over an open fire.
holidays.funFact: Paella originated in the Valencia region of Spain, and the word paella comes from the Latin word for pan, referring to the wide shallow cooking vessel used.
holidays.shareText: Gathering around the pan for a feast on National Spanish Paella Day! 🥘 #SpanishPaellaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Black Forest Cake Day
holidays.description: A rich celebration of the decadent German dessert featuring layers of chocolate cake, cherries, and whipped cream.
holidays.funFact: The Black Forest cake, known in German as Schwarzwalder Kirschtorte, is named after the Black Forest region of Germany and its specialty cherry brandy Kirschwasser.
holidays.shareText: Diving into layers of chocolatey, cherry perfection on National Black Forest Cake Day! 🎂 #BlackForestCakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Weed Appreciation Day
holidays.description: A day to reconsider our relationship with weeds and appreciate the ecological role these often overlooked plants play in nature.
holidays.funFact: Many plants we consider weeds, like dandelions and clover, are actually important food sources for pollinators like bees and butterflies.
holidays.shareText: Finding beauty in the unexpected on Weed Appreciation Day! 🌼 #WeedAppreciationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lemon Chiffon Cake Day
holidays.description: A light and zesty celebration of the airy chiffon cake bursting with bright lemon flavor and a tender, cloud like crumb.
holidays.funFact: Chiffon cake was invented in 1927 by insurance salesman Harry Baker, who kept his secret recipe for vegetable oil in the batter hidden for 20 years.
holidays.shareText: Light, fluffy, and full of sunshine on National Lemon Chiffon Cake Day! 🍋 #LemonChiffonCakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Mom and Pop Business Owners Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate and support the independent small business owners who are the backbone of local communities.
holidays.funFact: Small businesses account for 99.9 percent of all US businesses and employ nearly half of the American private sector workforce.
holidays.shareText: Showing love for local small businesses on National Mom and Pop Business Owners Day! 🏪 #MomAndPopBusinessOwnersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Take a Walk in the Park Day
holidays.description: An invitation to step outside, breathe fresh air, and enjoy the beauty of a local park as spring arrives in full force.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that spending just 20 minutes walking in a park or natural setting can significantly reduce stress hormones and improve mood.
holidays.shareText: Lacing up my shoes and heading outside on Take a Walk in the Park Day! 🌳 #WalkInTheParkDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Doctors Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the physicians and medical professionals who dedicate their lives to healing and caring for others.
holidays.funFact: National Doctors Day in the US is celebrated on March 30 to commemorate the anniversary of the first use of general anesthesia in surgery by Dr. Crawford Long in 1842.
holidays.shareText: Thanking the incredible doctors who keep us healthy on National Doctors Day! 🩺 #NationalDoctorsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Clams on the Half Shell Day
holidays.description: A briny celebration of fresh raw clams served on the half shell, a classic seafood tradition beloved by ocean food lovers.
holidays.funFact: Clams are one of the longest lived animals on Earth; an ocean quahog clam named Ming was found to be 507 years old when it was discovered in 2006.
holidays.shareText: Shucking and savoring on National Clams on the Half Shell Day! 🦪 #ClamsOnTheHalfShellDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Backup Day
holidays.description: An annual reminder to back up your important data and protect yourself from data loss before April Fools Day.
holidays.funFact: About 30 percent of people have never backed up their data, and 60 percent of companies that lose their data shut down within 6 months.
holidays.shareText: Backing up my files today because losing data is no joke! Happy World Backup Day! 💾 #WorldBackupDay #CelebriDay
